title: min()
description: min() picks the smallest of its arguments. Most useful for responsive widths that fill available space but never exceed a maximum — without a media query.
html: This container fills its parent but stops growing at 600px. Try resizing the preview.
challenge: Make the container fill its parent's width but never exceed 600px — using a single min() expression for the width property, without adding max-width.

alternatives.name: max() for minimum font sizes
alternatives.description: max() picks the largest value — useful for a font size that grows with the viewport but never drops below a readable minimum. font-size: max(1rem, 2.5vw) scales up on large screens and floors at 1rem on small ones.

realWorldUsage: Nearly every centered page layout uses this pattern — the content wrapper should fill narrow screens but stop growing on wide ones. min(100%, 1200px) paired with margin-inline: auto is the modern equivalent of the classic width: 100%; max-width: 1200px; margin: 0 auto combination.
